stories.title: CPSC April 23 Recall Batch — Wiifo Tower Stool + HappyGira Baby Lounger

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
CPSC alert: Two new recalls this week.

Wiifo tower stool (Amazon, ~$60): can collapse; child's torso can fit through the sides.
HappyGira baby lounger: suffocation and fall risk.

Stop use immediately.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
If you have a Wiifo tower stool (model LT005) from Amazon or a HappyGira baby lounger — both were recalled by CPSC on April 23.

Stop use, request a full refund.

Details at cpsc.gov

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Two product recalls from the CPSC this week that COS parents need on their radar. 🚨

The first: Wiifo Children's Tower Stool, model LT005. About 9,700 of these were sold on Amazon between June 2022 and March 2026 for around $60. The problem — the stool can collapse or tip over while in use, and a child's torso can fit through the openings on the sides. That's an entrapment and fall hazard that the CPSC has classified as serious. If you have one, stop using it immediately and contact the seller for a full refund.

The second: HappyGira baby loungers — the Sweetie Baby and Style Life Eleven styles. Sold on HappyGira.com between September 2025 and February 2026 for $69.99–$99.99. The sides are shorter than required by federal safety standards, there's no stand, and the sleeping pad is thicker than allowed — creating fall, entrapment, and suffocation hazards. Again: stop use immediately and reach out to HappyGira for a full refund.

Both recalls were officially posted by the CPSC on April 23, 2026. Photos and details are at cpsc.gov. The safest move is to err on the side of caution, even if you haven't noticed any issues.

Have you found any recalled products in your home recently? How do you stay on top of baby and kids' product safety news?

---

#### Image Caption -- 8:00 AM MT
facebook.image_caption: Two new CPSC recalls — Wiifo tower stool and HappyGira baby lounger — posted April 23. If you have either at home, stop use immediately. Details at cpsc.gov 🚨

article.headline: Stop Use Now: CPSC Issues Two New Recalls on Tower Stool and Baby Lounger

stories.title: "3 Days 3 Parks" Earth Day Weekend Volunteer Cleanup — April 24–26

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
This weekend (April 24-26): Colorado Springs Parks is hosting 3 Days 3 Parks — a family volunteer event at Blodgett, Palmer Park, and Ute Valley.

Trail repair. Weed removal. Free for all ages.

3days3parks.org

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
We're big fans of anything that gets families outdoors *and* makes COS a better place. This weekend's "3 Days 3 Parks" event checks both boxes. 🌿

The Colorado Springs Parks Department is hosting its 5th annual Earth Day volunteer cleanup from Friday, April 24 through Sunday, April 26. Families can volunteer at any of three parks: Blodgett Open Space, Palmer Park, and Ute Valley Park. The work includes trail repair, invasive plant removal, trash pickup, and reseeding. It's genuinely satisfying outdoor time — and it's something kids can participate in and feel proud of.

The event is free. All ages are welcome. Registration is at 3days3parks.org, and you can sign up for whichever park and time works best for your family's schedule.

COS got more than 22.5 million park visits in 2025. These spaces belong to all of us — and this weekend is a chance to give a little back.

Which of the three parks does your family love most?

---

#### Image Caption -- 12:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: COS families — this weekend is 3 Days 3 Parks (April 24-26). Trail repair, weed removal, trash cleanup at Blodgett, Palmer Park, and Ute Valley. Free for all ages. Sign up at 3days3parks.org 🌿

stories.title: D49 Appoints New Board Member — Jack Ryan Fills Vacant Seat

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
D49 families: your school board has a new member.

Jack Ryan — retired Army Sergeant Major, Senior IT Project Manager, 5 years in Peyton — was unanimously appointed to the D49 Board of Education on April 20.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
D49 families, your school board is now complete again. 📋

Colorado Springs School District 49 filled its vacant board seat on April 20, 2026 with the unanimous appointment of Jack Ryan. Ryan is a retired U.S. Army Sergeant Major and a Senior IT Project Manager who's called Peyton home for the past five years. The appointment was made at a special board meeting.

D49 serves families across the eastern Colorado Springs area and has faced significant financial and planning challenges over the past year. Board membership matters — decisions on curriculum, staffing, budget, and school safety all flow through this group of seven.

If you're a D49 parent or guardian, now is a good time to check when the next open board meeting is. Showing up — even just to observe — keeps the lines of accountability healthy.

What would you most want D49's board to prioritize in the remainder of the 2025-26 school year?

---

stories.title: Research Roundup — Outdoor Play Makes Kids Sleep Better

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text:
Research reminder: outdoor play helps kids sleep better.

Natural light regulates circadian rhythms. Even 30-60 minutes outside can make a real difference at bedtime.

COS has 22.5 million park visits/year for a reason.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text:
1 in 10 toddlers and preschoolers plays outside once a week or less — per a U-M Mott Children's Hospital poll.

The research is clear: more outside time = better sleep.

What's your family's go-to COS outdoor spot?

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Here's a parenting tip that's both obvious and easy to forget when life gets busy: getting kids outside more helps them sleep better at night. 💤

A national poll by the University of Michigan C.S. Mott Children's Hospital found that 1 in 10 toddlers and preschoolers plays outside just once a week or less. That's a surprisingly low number — especially when research consistently shows that active outdoor time, particularly exposure to natural light, helps regulate children's circadian rhythms and improve sleep quality.

The connection is real: outdoor play leads to physical tiredness, natural light cues the body's internal clock, and the reduction in screen time that comes with outdoor activity removes a major sleep disruptor. A CNN report from March 2026 put it plainly — when kids don't sleep, parents don't either.

COS is genuinely one of the best cities in the country for accessible outdoor time. More than 22.5 million park visits happened here in 2025. This weekend alone, there are trail cleanups at three parks, accessible hikes launching at Fox Run, and countless trails open across the city.

What's your family's go-to outdoor routine when bedtime is struggling? We'd love to know what works for you.

---

#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM MT
facebook.image_caption: Research says outdoor play = better sleep for kids. Natural light regulates circadian rhythms, active play creates healthy tiredness, and less screen time means fewer sleep disruptions. COS is full of options. 💤

article.headline: Science Confirms It: Getting Kids Outside Helps Them Sleep — And COS Has the Space to Make It Easy

stories.title: Fox Run Regional Park Gets Free Accessible Trail Program — Starting May 1

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Fox Run Regional Park is launching free accessible trail hikes starting May 1.

A Terrain Hopper guides participants of all abilities on 3-mile hikes. Saturdays, May 1–Oct 31.

Free. Register: parks.elpasoco.com

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
We don't talk enough about how COS parks serve families of ALL abilities — but this new program deserves a spotlight. 🌲

Starting May 1, 2026, El Paso County Parks is launching the Trailability Program at Fox Run Regional Park (2110 Stella Drive). The program uses a Terrain Hopper — a specialized vehicle secured through a grant from the Colorado Disability Funding Committee — to guide participants of all physical abilities on hikes up to 3 miles through Fox Run's forested trails.

The program is completely free. It runs Saturdays, both mornings and afternoons, from May 1 through October 31. Trained volunteers familiar with the Fox Run route will guide each group. This is a real gift for families who've wanted to access natural spaces but faced mobility barriers.

Registration is open now at parks.elpasoco.com. If you know a family who would benefit, this is worth sharing.

What accessible outdoor options in COS have been important to your family?

---
